instruction: Classify the contract clause into its clause type.
baseline_prompt: You are a contract-clause classifier. Classify the contract clause into its clause type. Classify into one of these categories: indemnification, termination, confidentiality, governing_law, limitation_of_liability, payment_terms.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: Supplier shall be liable to Purchaser for any and all losses, damages, and expenses, including court costs and attorney fees, sustained by Purchaser as a result of injury to persons or property caused by defective materials or workmanship provided by Supplier under this Purchase Order.

This Agreement shall automatically expire on December 31, 2025, unless extended by mutual written consent of both parties or unless earlier dissolved in accordance with the provisions set forth herein.

Employee acknowledges that in the course of employment, Employee will have access to sensitive company information including customer lists, pricing strategies, marketing plans, and product development roadmaps, and agrees to keep all such information strictly confidential both during and after the term of employment.

For all purposes of this Contract, the parties hereby submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Singapore, and the rights and obligations of the parties shall be determined in accordance with the laws of Singapore.

Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Service Provider's liability for direct damages shall be limited to fifty thousand dollars ($50,000) in the aggregate, and Service Provider expressly disclaims all liability for any indirect, exemplary, or punitive damages arising from performance or non-performance under this Agreement.

Contractor agrees to bill the Company bi-weekly based on hours worked at the rate of $125 per hour. All invoices must be submitted by the 15th and last day of each month, with remittance due within 15 business days of receipt.

The Service Provider hereby agrees to compensate and shield the Customer from any financial loss, damage, or expense, including but not limited to legal fees, that may result from Service Provider's violation of applicable data protection regulations or unauthorized disclosure of Customer information.

Should either party wish to exit this business relationship, a minimum of ninety (90) days advance written notification must be provided, during which time all ongoing projects shall be brought to completion.

During the term of this Agreement and for a period of five years thereafter, each party shall maintain in strict confidence all information marked as proprietary or that would reasonably be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure.

The Parties agree that Swiss law shall govern all aspects of this contractual relationship, and any legal proceedings shall be conducted in accordance with the substantive law of Switzerland, with the exclusion of conflict of law rules.

The parties agree that Vendor's entire liability for all claims of any kind relating to the services provided shall be capped at an amount equal to two times the annual service fees, and this cap shall apply regardless of whether the claim is based on contract, negligence, strict liability, or any other legal theory.

All fees are quoted in U.S. dollars and must be paid via wire transfer to the account specified in Exhibit A. Service Provider reserves the right to suspend services if any invoice remains outstanding for more than 60 days.

Should any regulatory authority impose fines, penalties, or sanctions upon Owner arising from Operator's failure to comply with environmental laws and regulations in the operation of the facility, Operator shall reimburse Owner for the full amount of such fines, penalties, and all related legal expenses within thirty days of written demand.
Upon dissolution of this arrangement, all outstanding deliverables must be completed within fifteen business days, and all proprietary materials shall be returned to the disclosing party.

The Receiving Party agrees not to disclose, publish, or disseminate any proprietary information, trade secrets, technical data, or business strategies obtained from the Disclosing Party to any third party without prior written consent, and shall take all reasonable measures to protect such information from unauthorized access or use.

test.query: This Agreement and all matters arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be governed by the federal laws of the United States and the laws of the State of New York applicable to contracts entered into and performed entirely within New York.

In no event shall either party be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of profits, loss of revenue, loss of data, or business interruption, regardless of the theory of liability, whether arising under contract, tort, or otherwise, even if such party has been advised of the possibility of such damages.

The Client shall remit full compensation within thirty (30) days of invoice date. Invoices will be issued monthly on the first business day of each month for services rendered during the preceding month. Late payments shall accrue interest at a rate of 1.5% per month.

Consultant will protect Company from financial harm and assume responsibility for any damages awarded against Company in connection with bodily injury or property damage to the extent such injury or damage is caused by Consultant's negligent acts or omissions in performing the services described herein.

In the event of acquisition, merger, or change of control affecting either party, the non-affected party shall have the option to discontinue this Agreement without penalty upon sixty (60) days notice.

All technical specifications, designs, drawings, reports, and analyses provided under this Agreement are considered confidential and proprietary. Recipient shall not reproduce, reverse engineer, or create derivative works based on such materials without express written authorization.

The parties agree that the substantive laws of England and Wales shall apply to any disputes arising out of or relating to this Contract, excluding any rules of private international law that would lead to the application of any other law.

Supplier shall not be liable for damages exceeding the purchase price of the specific goods that are the subject of the claim, and in no case shall Supplier be liable for incidental or consequential damages of any nature, including without limitation, lost profits or revenues.

Licensee shall pay an annual licensing fee of $25,000, due on January 1st of each calendar year. Additionally, Licensee owes a royalty of 5% of net revenues from licensed products, calculated and paid quarterly within 45 days after quarter end.

In the event that any action, lawsuit, or claim is brought against Licensee by a third party alleging that the Product infringes any intellectual property rights, Licensor shall assume full responsibility for the defense of such claim and shall pay all judgments, settlements, and legal fees incurred.
Either party may cancel this Agreement upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party if a material breach remains uncured after the notice period has elapsed.

Consultant shall not, during or after the consulting period, reveal to any person or entity any confidential knowledge or data concerning the Company's operations, clients, financial condition, business relationships, or any other information not available to the general public that was acquired during the provision of services hereunder.

The validity, interpretation, and performance of this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the internal laws of the State of California, excluding its choice of law provisions.

Under no circumstances shall Provider be responsible for any loss of business, loss of anticipated savings, loss of goodwill, or any form of special, indirect, or consequential loss or damage whatsoever, even where such loss was reasonably foreseeable or Provider had been advised of the possibility thereof.
The purchase price for the goods is $175,000 FOB origin. Buyer shall pay 40% deposit upon order confirmation, 40% prior to shipment, and the final 20% within seven days of delivery and inspection.

The Contractor shall defend, hold harmless, and reimburse the Company for any damages, costs, expenses, or liabilities arising from any claims, suits, or proceedings related to the Contractor's breach of this Agreement or negligent performance of services hereunder, including reasonable attorneys' fees.
This contract may be dissolved by mutual agreement of the parties at any time, provided that such dissolution is documented in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both entities.

The parties recognize that disclosure of confidential business information could cause irreparable harm. Each party therefore agrees to implement appropriate safeguards including password protection, encryption where applicable, and physical security measures to prevent unauthorized disclosure or access to confidential materials.
